content: Which of the following is the most reactive towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ mechanism?
explanation:
$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ mechanism depends on carbocation stability. The more stable the carbocation formed after the leaving group departs, the faster is the $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction.

In

tertiary benzylic carbocation is stabilised by $+I$ effect of $\mathrm{CH}_3$ and 2 phenyl group, which is most stable among the given options.

content: The organic halide, which does not undergo hydrolysis by $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ mechanism is

options.content: $\mathrm{CH}_{2}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}_{2} \mathrm{Cl}$
$\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3}\right)_{3} \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{Cl}$
$\mathrm{CH}_{3}-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{Cl}$

explanation:
Among the given options $ \mathrm{CH}_{3}-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{Cl} $ does not undergo $ \mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1 $ mechanism. It is because the carbocation (vinylic)

$ \mathrm{CH}_{3}-\mathrm{CH}=\stackrel{\oplus}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H} \text { is very unstable. } $

content: The reagent ' $X$ ' used in the following reaction to obtain good yield of the product is

options.content: $\mathrm{KI}, \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$
$\mathrm{KI}, 95 \% \mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4}$
$\mathrm{Nal}, \mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$
HI

explanation:
The reagent used to obtained good yield of product is $ \mathrm{KI}, 95 \% \mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4} $.

Alcohol can be converted to alkyl halides using KI in presence of

$ \mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4} $. $ R-\mathrm{OH}+\mathrm{HI} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4}]{\mathrm{KI}} R \mathrm{I}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} $

content:
Identify the major product $(P)$ in the following reaction sequence.

$$ \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr} \xrightarrow[\text { KOH, } \Delta]{\text { Alcoholic }} X \xrightarrow{\mathrm{HBr}} P $$

options.content: $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr}$
$\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{CHCH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$
$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$

explanation:
The complete reaction is as follows,

$$ \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr} \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\text { Alc. } \mathrm{KOH}} \underset{\substack{\text { (Product) } \\\\ X}}{\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_2} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{HBr}} \underset{\substack{\text { (Product) } \\\\\left(\mathrm{Y}\right)}}{\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr}} $$

content:
What is the percentage of carbon in the product ' $X$ ' formed in the given reaction?

$+\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{Cl} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{AlCl}_3]{\text { Anhydrous }} X$
explanation:
The reaction mentioned is Friedel-Crafts reaction.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { Ethyl benzene }=\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3 \\ & \text { Total mass }=8 \times 12+10 \times 1=106 \mathrm{~g} \\ & \text { Percentage of carbon }=\frac{96}{106} \times 100 \\ & \text { = 90.6\% } \end{aligned} $$

content: Which of the following is an example of allylic halide?
explanation: Allylic halides are the compounds in which the halogen group is attached to a hybridised carbon $\left(s p^3\right)$ atom next to the carbon which is already in double bond with another carbon.

content: A halogen compound $X\left(\mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_9 \mathrm{Br}\right)$ on hydrolysis gave alcohol $Y$. The alcohol $Y$ undergoes dehydration with $20 \% \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ at 358 K . What is $X$ ?

options.content: $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr}$
$\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{CHCH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}(\mathrm{Br}) \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3$

explanation: This is an example of $S_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction. The complete reaction is as follows.

content: Identify the halogen exchange reaction from the following.

options.content: Sandmeyer reaction
Swarts reaction
Stephen reaction
Wurtz reaction

explanation:
Swarts reaction is a halogen exchange reaction. It is used to get alkyl fluorides from alkyl chlorides or bromides.

$$ R-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{AgF} \longrightarrow R-\mathrm{F}+\mathrm{AgCl} $$

content: Which of the following is least reactive towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reactions?

options.content: $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}$
$\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{Cl}$
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}$
$\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5\right)_2 \mathrm{CHCl}$

explanation: Among the given options, chlorobenzene is least reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reaction due to the delocalisation of the electrons of $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond and a partial double bond character developed in the bond which makes it difficult for nucleophile to deave $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond.

content: An alkyl halide $X\left(\mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_9 \mathrm{Br}\right)$ undergoes nucleophilic substitution by $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction. The product of $X$ on reaction with $\mathrm{Mg} /$ dry ether followed by $\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is
explanation: $$ \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Br} \xrightarrow[\mathrm{Mg} / \text { Dry ether }]{\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}} \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{D}$$

content: Choose the correct decreasing order of reactivity of alkyl halides towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction.

options.content: Primary halide $>$ secondary halide $>$ tertiary halide
Secondary halide $>$ tertiary halide $>$ primary halide
Tertiary halide $>$ secondary halide $>$ primary halide
Tertiary halide $>$ primary halide $>$ secondary halide

explanation:
For $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction, carbocation intermediate is formed and stability of carbocation defines feasibility of reaction. The order of stability of carbocation is tertiary > secondary > primary

Order of reactivity of alkyl halide is tertiary halide $>$ secondary halide $>$ primary halide.

content: Which of the following reaction represents swarts reaction?
explanation:
Swart's reaction is used for synthesis of alkyl fluorides from alkyl bromides.

$$ \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Br} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{CoF}_2} \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{~F} $$

content: Which of the following is least reactive towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction?
explanation:
The order of reactivity towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction for alkyl halide is methyl $>$ primary halide $\left(1^{\circ}\right)>$ secondary halide $\left(2^{\circ}\right)>$ tertiary halide $\left(3^{\circ}\right)$.

Thus, option (d) is least reactive towards $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction as it is a $3^{\circ}$ halide.

2-methylbutene
n-pentane
$n$-pentene

content: The major products $Y$ and $Z$ in the following reactions are

$$ \text { Match the following. } $$

$$ \begin{array}{llll} \hline \text { (A) } & \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \xrightarrow{\mathrm{Aq} . \mathrm{KOH}} & \text { I } & \mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1 \\ \hline \text { (B) } & \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{Cl} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}} & \text { II } & \mathrm{E} 2 \\ \hline \text { (C) } & \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \xrightarrow{\text { Alc. } \mathrm{KOH}} & \text { III } & \mathrm{E} 1 \\ \hline \text { (D) } & \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{COH} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{H}^{+}} & \text {IV } & \mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2 \\ \hline \end{array} $$

$$ \text { The correct match is } $$

options.content:
A B C D
I II III IV

A B C D
IV II III I

A B C D
IV I II III

A B C D
IV II I III

explanation:
Primary halides undergo $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction with $\mathrm{aq} . \mathrm{KOH}$. The reaction forms a transition state where the attacking nucleophile and leaving group both are partially bonded to carbon.

$3^{\circ}$ alkyl halide undergoes nucleophilic substitution reaction via formation of a stable $3^{\circ}$ carbocation. Thus, it shows $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction where rate of reaction depends only on concentration of substrate.

$$ \text { (C) } \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{I} \xrightarrow{\text { Alc. } \mathrm{KOH}}\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}_2 $$

Primary halides prefer to undergo E2 elimination reaction.

The reaction path followed is E1 reaction.

∴ The correct option is $\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{IV}, \mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{I}, \mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{II}$ and $\mathrm{D} \rightarrow \mathrm{III}$.

explanation:
Due to presence of peroxide, addition of HBr takes place according to anti-Markownikoff rule in which negative part of addendum goes to carbon which has more number of H -atom attached to it.

Hydrolysis of cyano group will yield or produce.

content: $$ \text { The major product in the following reaction is } $$
explanation:
(i) Firstly toluene reacts with bromine in the presence of light to give benzyl bromide. This reaction is free radical substitution reaction.

(ii) Benzyl bromide reacts with magnesium to give benzyl magnesium bromide (Grignard reagent).

(iii), (iv) In next step, benzyl magnesium bromide reacts with formaldehyde $\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)$ followed by acidic hydrolysis to give 2-phenylethanol.

(v) In last step, alcohol is converted to alkyl halide in presence of $\mathrm{PBr}_3$.

content: $$ \text { The major product of the following reactions is } $$
explanation:
Mechanism involved

Step (i) Bromination reaction Electrophilic substitution takes place.

Step (ii) Friedel crafts alkylation Electrophilic substitution takes place. As, Br is ortho-para directing group, so, alkylation occurs at para-position.

$$ \text { Step (iii) Grignard reagent formation } $$

Step (iv) Nucleophilic addition reaction of Grignard reagent

$$ \text { Step (v) Hydrolysis } $$

content:
The order of reactivity of the following compounds towards dilute aqueous KOH in $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction is
I. $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$
II. $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{CCH}_2 \mathrm{CHBrCH}_3$
III. $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right) \mathrm{CHCH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$
IV. $\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr}$

options.content: I $<$ IV $<$ III $<$ II
IV $<$ II $<$ III $<$ I
III $<$ II $<$ I $<$ IV
I $<$ III $<$ II $<$ IV

explanation:
The $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction proceed by the formation of carbonium ion. The order of stability of carbonium ion is as follows

$$ 3^{\circ}>2^{\circ}>1^{\circ} $$

Also, more the number of carbon atoms, more will be the stability of carbonium ion. Therefore, the correct order of reactivity of $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reaction is as follows

$$ \text { I }<\text { III }<\text { II }<\text { IV } $$

content: $$ \text { The major product in the following reaction sequence is } $$

Step 1 First of all cyclohexanone react with Et MgBr and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$.

Step 2 In the presence of $20 \% \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ elimination undergoes and water is removed resulting alkene is formed.

Step 3 HBr react with alkene forms haloderivative.
